header_summary:
Bitcoin Core version 31.1 has been launched, introducing significant improvements such as bug fixes, performance upgrades, and newer system compatibility (Linux Kernel 3.17+, macOS 14+, Windows 10 version 1903+). Noteworthy enhancements include resolving a chainstate database issue and privacy upgrades like IP address protection during private broadcasts. Users are urged to download the update from [Bitcoin Core's official website](https://bitcoincore.org/bin/bitcoin-core-31.1/) and report any issues on [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues). 

Recent advancements in zero-knowledge (zk) technologies within the Bitcoin ecosystem are drawing attention due to their potential to enhance privacy and efficiency in blockchain technology. This progress is seen as a promising development for the blockchain sector, focusing on security improvements that could benefit a wide user base. More on these innovations can be found on [Delving Bitcoin](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/zkpoh-zero-knowledge-proof-of-hodl/2699/3).

A proposed ecash payout system for small miners could revolutionize payout management in mining pools by setting a limit on direct payouts and redirecting smaller balances to an ecash mint for distribution. This system aims to reduce risks like dust attacks and dependency on custodial solutions, although it faces challenges such as high transaction fees for smaller payouts and technical hurdles in achieving decentralized financial practices. Further details are discussed on [Delving Bitcoin](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/fee-selection-for-ctv-based-mining-pool-fanouts/2698/6).

The consolidation of miners and the movement towards centralization within the Bitcoin network is raising concerns about the cryptocurrency's security and decentralized nature. Discussions on potential solutions, including increasing miner subsidies, are ongoing, with community engagement encouraged on platforms like [BitcoinTalk](https://bitcointalk.org/index.php?topic=5587984.0), where various aspects of these challenges are explored.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core v31.1 Released
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core version 31.1 is now available, featuring key improvements and bug fixes.
- Update procedures vary by system; complete shutdown of older versions is necessary.
- The release addresses significant technical issues, enhancing functionality and security.

recent_posts.title: zkPoH: Zero-Knowledge Proof-of-Hodl
recent_posts.summary:
- Recent developments in zk technologies have notably advanced the Bitcoin ecosystem.
- Growing interest in zk technologies signifies their impact on Bitcoin's security and capabilities.
- These advancements suggest promising directions for blockchain focusing on privacy and efficiency.

recent_posts.title: Fee Selection for CTV-Based Mining Pool Fanouts
recent_posts.summary:
- An innovative ecash payout system for miners is proposed, still in early development.
- The system segregates payouts to reduce risks and addresses miner interactions with blockchain.
- It aims to foster decentralized financial practices despite current technological and economic barriers.

recent_posts.title: Addressing the Diminishing Block Subsidy
recent_posts.summary:
- Miner consolidation in Bitcoin threatens its decentralized nature and security.
- Increasing subsidies for miners may not solve centralization and shrinking security budgets.
- Active discussions on BitcoinTalk explore solutions and implications of this issue.

active_posts.title: DahLIAS: Discrete Logarithm-Based Interactive Aggregate Signatures
active_posts.summary:
- DahLIAS introduces a constant-size signature scheme compatible with secp256k1 for CISA.
- It overcomes past challenges with secure and efficient frameworks in cryptographic protocols.
- DahLIAS features enhanced verification speed and a two-round signing process.

active_posts.title: Giving teeth to expected EC disabling: P2XX(-T)(-ML)
active_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin developers explore new security measures for output types like P2TRv2.
- Two primary proposals, Tripwire and Miner Lockdown, aim to enhance network security.
- These integrations increase complexity but align with cryptocurrency's decentralized ethos.

active_posts.title: BIP draft: Full-Aggregation of BIP 340 Signatures
active_posts.summary: - A new Bitcoin Improvement Proposal introduces efficient signature aggregation methods.  - The proposal is accessible for review and feedback on GitHub through mock pull requests.  - This method aims to enhance security and efficiency in Bitcoin transactions.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
active_posts.summary:
- SegData, following BIP-141, introduces prunable, script-isolated data blocks within Bitcoin.
- It is designed to shift storage costs to operators, allowing optional data pruning post-validation.
- Potential users include indexers and timestamping services, with open questions on implementation details.

active_posts.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal aims to optimize BIP-360 by reducing EC witness size without increasing quantum exposure.
- It eliminates EC public key scripts, using a recoverable leaf for deriving the key from signatures.
- This approach ensures smaller, secure witnesses and integrates well with current BIP-360 frameworks.

active_posts.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
active_posts.summary:
- Transaction privacy in Bitcoin focuses on sidechains, L2s, and wallet integrations.
- Cross-Input Signature Aggregation may reduce costs but not enhance privacy significantly.
- Current privacy enhancements prioritize regulatory compatibility over protocol-level changes.

today_in_history_posts.title: Weekly IRC Meeting Time Poll
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- A Bitcoin developer has requested participation in a poll on meeting timings.
- The poll aims to reschedule weekly IRC meetings for broader inclusivity.
- Poll results will conclude on July 19, during the scheduled IRC meeting.

today_in_history_posts.title: proposal for Lightning Network improvement proposals
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- A proposal initiated the creation of Lightning Network Improvement Proposals (LIPs).
- LIP-0001 draft has been made, outlining the process similar to BIP-0002.
- Rene Pickhardt has started working on a LIP for Splicing.

today_in_history_posts.title: Deflationary money is a Good Thing
today_in_history_posts.summary: - Passive and active investments are the two primary investment strategies.  - Passive investment faces issues like asset underutilization due to principal/agent conflicts.  - Active investment benefits in deflationary settings by promoting asset development and utilization.
